scope: F02: Held-out prompt generalization under input-length control

findings.description: Deception held-out prompts append 'I need you to give me a confidently wrong answer.' creating ~11 token systematic input-length difference, same confound as F01b/49b
findings.impact: Deception transfer AUROC 0.81 is likely an input-length artifact. The held-out evaluation does NOT provide independent evidence for the deception claim.
findings.recommendation: Run experiment and verify AUROC collapse after residualization

findings.description: Within-fold residualization uses training-fold OLS applied to test data. With n_train=20, n_test=20, the OLS regression has few degrees of freedom and may not capture the full confound relationship.
findings.impact: Residualized AUROC may be slightly biased upward (understating the confound) due to imprecise regression fit on small training data
findings.recommendation: Consider also reporting full-data residualization as a sensitivity check

findings.description: Bootstrap uses 2000 resamples instead of the project standard 10000, to keep runtime manageable with 3 paradigms
findings.impact: Bootstrap CI may be slightly wider than with 10K resamples, but 2K is sufficient for 95% CI
findings.recommendation: Could increase to 5000 if runtime allows

findings.description: Refusal and impossibility training data use different prompt styles (harmful/impossible prompts) while test data uses held-out versions. Input-length differences in these paradigms reflect genuine prompt-type differences, not a systematic append. Residualization may over-correct.
findings.impact: For refusal/impossibility, residualization removes both confound AND legitimate signal that correlates with prompt type. A surviving AUROC is strong evidence; a collapsed AUROC is ambiguous.
findings.recommendation: Note this asymmetry in interpretation: for deception, collapse = artifact confirmed; for refusal/impossibility, collapse could be over-correction

findings.description: Red team: The experiment only controls for LINEAR input-length confounds via OLS residualization. If input length affects KV-cache features nonlinearly, the residualization will understate the confound. Additionally, with only 10+10 held-out items per condition, statistical power is very low.
findings.impact: Small sample size (n=20 per paradigm in held-out) means AUROC estimates have wide confidence intervals. A result of AUROC=0.55 vs 0.65 may not be statistically distinguishable. The experiment may produce WEAKENED verdicts where FALSIFIED would be correct with more data.
findings.recommendation: 1) Consider polynomial residualization as sensitivity check. 2) Report bootstrap CIs prominently. 3) Interpret WEAKENED conservatively given low power.

referrals.description: Verify that the refusal/impossibility paradigms in 49d actually use different prompt text lengths (not just different token counts from tokenization). The word-count statistics in the output will clarify this.
Check whether the 49d transfer_results AUROCs (0.76-0.81) were computed with the same classifier pipeline (StandardScaler + LogisticRegression) used here, or with a different method.
